differential_diagnoses: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system (PACNS)
Cerebral amyloid angiopathy with superficial siderosis
next_steps: Brain and leptomeningeal biopsy of the most prominent nodular/hyperintense lesions
Whole-body PET/CT imaging to search for occult primary melanoma
Dermatologic examination with dermoscopy for cutaneous melanocytic lesions
Dilated funduscopic examination for intraocular melanocytic lesions
Immunohistochemistry (MITF, SOX10, Melan-A) and GNAQ/GNA11 mutation analysis of biopsy tissue
BRAF V600E mutation testing on biopsy tissue to distinguish between primary CNS and metastatic melanoma
MRI of the spine to assess for diffuse neuraxial involvement
